revision.comment: 新しいページ：北塚古墳（広島県福山市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 北塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 34|緯度秒 = 22.38 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 18|経度秒 = 55.30 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 組合式[[家形石棺]]
（[[横口式石槨]]?） |出土品 = 鉄釘・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「北塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = |ラベル = 北塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''北塚古墳'''（きたづかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷にある[[古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 広島県東部、[[神辺平野]]から北に入った、服部大池から北の谷に面する丘陵端に築造された古墳である。これまでに墳丘は削平で失われているほか、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）に試掘調査が実施されている[http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。 元々の墳形は明らかでない。主体部の埋葬施設としては[[花崗岩]]製の組合式[[家形石棺]]（または[[横口式石槨]]の石槨部）が遺存する。6枚の切石を組み合わせた石棺で、長さ2.34メートル・幅1.41メートル。高さ0.56メートルを測る。蓋石は各辺が丸みを帯びた長方形で、縄掛突起が退化したような装飾が造り出される。出土品としては試掘の際の鉄釘・[[須恵器]]（坏）片がある。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半史跡説明板。（または7世紀前半{{Sfn|北塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}）頃と推定され、[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）のような[[横口式石槨]]（石棺式石室）古墳の前段階に位置づけられる。 古墳域は[[1988年]]（昭和63年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 北塚古墳 - 1988年（昭和63年）12月26日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会、2013年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 35 広島県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|北塚古墳（平凡社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|北塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:きたつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 北塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]

（[[横口式石槨]]?） |出土品 = 鉄釘・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「北塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = |ラベル = 北塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''北塚古墳'''（きたづかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷にある[[古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|家形石棺}}{{small|[[横口式石槨]]の石槨部とする説もある。}}]] 広島県東部、[[神辺平野]]から北に入った、服部大池から北の谷に面する丘陵端に築造された古墳である。これまでに墳丘は削平で失われているほか、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）に試掘調査が実施されている[http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。 元々の墳形は明らかでない。主体部の埋葬施設としては[[花崗岩]]製の組合式[[家形石棺]]（または[[横口式石槨]]の石槨部）が遺存する。6枚の切石を組み合わせた石棺で、長さ2.34メートル・幅1.41メートル。高さ0.56メートルを測る。蓋石は各辺が丸みを帯びた長方形で、縄掛突起が退化したような装飾が造り出される。出土品としては試掘の際の鉄釘・[[須恵器]]（坏）片がある。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半史跡説明板。（または7世紀前半{{Sfn|北塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}）頃と推定され、[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）のような[[横口式石槨]]（石棺式石室）古墳の前段階に位置づけられる。 古墳域は[[1988年]]（昭和63年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|石棺横景 File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|石棺蓋石
{{small|左側に縄掛突起状装飾が造り出される。}} File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|石棺内部
{{small|蓋石内面・側石小口側に彫込が認められる。}} == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 北塚古墳 - 1988年（昭和63年）12月26日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会、2013年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 35 広島県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|北塚古墳（平凡社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|北塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [http://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:きたつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 http:// -> https:// (www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 北塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
[[家形石棺]] 遠景 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 34|緯度秒 = 22.38 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 18|経度秒 = 55.30 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 組合式[[家形石棺]]
（[[横口式石槨]]?） |出土品 = 鉄釘・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「北塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = |ラベル = 北塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''北塚古墳'''（きたづかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷にある[[古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|家形石棺俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|家形石棺展開図}}]] 広島県東部、[[神辺平野]]から北に入った、服部大池から北の谷に面する丘陵端に築造された古墳である。墳丘は削平で失われているほか、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）に試掘調査が実施されている[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。 元々の墳形は明らかでない。埋葬施設としては[[花崗岩]]製の組合式[[家形石棺]]（または[[横口式石槨]]の石槨部）が遺存する。6枚の切石を組み合わせた石棺で、長さ2.34メートル・幅1.41メートル。高さ0.56メートルを測る。蓋石は各辺が丸みを帯びた長方形で、縄掛突起が退化したような装飾を造り出す。出土品としては試掘の際の鉄釘・[[須恵器]]（坏）片がある。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半史跡説明板。（または7世紀前半{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}）頃と推定され、[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）のような[[横口式石槨]]（石棺式石室）古墳の前段階に位置づけられる。 古墳域は[[1988年]]（昭和63年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|石棺横景 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|石棺蓋石
{{small|左側に縄掛突起状装飾が造り出される。}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|石棺内部
{{small|蓋石内面・側石小口側に彫込が認められる。}} == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 北塚古墳 - 1988年（昭和63年）12月26日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会、2013年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:きたつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ** Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[ja:北塚古墳]]

revision.text:
'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin

'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:北塚古墳]]

'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin

'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 北塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
[[家形石棺]] 遠景 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 34|緯度秒 = 22.38 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 18|経度秒 = 55.30 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 組合式[[家形石棺]]
（[[横口式石槨]]?） |出土品 = 鉄釘・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「北塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = |ラベル = 北塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''北塚古墳'''（きたづかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷にある[[古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-C.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|家形石棺立断面図}}]] 広島県東部、[[神辺平野]]から北に入った、服部大池から北の谷に面する丘陵端に築造された古墳である。墳丘は削平で失われているほか、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）に試掘調査が実施されている[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。 元々の墳形は明らかでない。埋葬施設としては[[花崗岩]]製の組合式[[家形石棺]]（または[[横口式石槨]]の石槨部）が遺存する。6枚の切石を組み合わせた石棺で、長さ2.34メートル・幅1.41メートル。高さ0.56メートルを測る。蓋石は各辺が丸みを帯びた長方形で、縄掛突起が退化したような装飾を造り出す。出土品としては試掘の際の鉄釘・[[須恵器]]（坏）片がある。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半史跡説明板。（または7世紀前半{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}）頃と推定され、[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）のような[[横口式石槨]]（石棺式石室）古墳の前段階に位置づけられる。 古墳域は[[1988年]]（昭和63年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|石棺横景 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|石棺蓋石
{{small|左側に縄掛突起状装飾が造り出される。}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|石棺内部
{{small|蓋石内面・側石小口側に彫込が認められる。}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|石棺俯瞰図 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|石棺展開図 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 北塚古墳 - 1988年（昭和63年）12月26日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会、2013年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:きたつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite revision.text:
'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). revision.text:
'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:11 deprecated open]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). revision.text:
'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=draft}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩|12=draft}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin

'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=draft}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩|12=draft}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[en:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[ja:北塚古墳]] [[sn:itazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitazuka Kofun}}

'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=draft}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩|12=draft}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[en:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[ja:北塚古墳]] [[sn:itazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitazuka Kofun}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 北塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
[[家形石棺]]開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 34|緯度秒 = 22.38 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 18|経度秒 = 55.30 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 組合式[[家形石棺]]（[[横口式石槨]]?） |出土品 = 鉄釘・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「北塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = |ラベル = 北塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''北塚古墳'''（きたづかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷にある[[古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-C.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|家形石棺立断面図}}]] 広島県東部、[[神辺平野]]から北に入った、服部大池から北の谷に面する丘陵端に築造された古墳である。墳丘は削平で失われているほか、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）に試掘調査が実施されている[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。 元々の墳形は明らかでない。埋葬施設としては[[花崗岩]]製の組合式[[家形石棺]]（または[[横口式石槨]]の石槨部）が遺存する。6枚の切石を組み合わせた石棺で、長さ2.34メートル・幅1.41メートル。高さ0.56メートルを測る。蓋石は各辺が丸みを帯びた長方形で、縄掛突起が退化したような装飾を造り出す。出土品としては試掘の際の鉄釘・[[須恵器]]（坏）片がある。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半史跡説明板。（または7世紀前半{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}）頃と推定され、[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）のような[[横口式石槨]]（石棺式石室）古墳の前段階に位置づけられる。 古墳域は[[1988年]]（昭和63年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|石棺横景 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|石棺蓋石
{{small|左側に縄掛突起状装飾を造り出す。}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|石棺内部
{{small|蓋石内面・側石小口側に彫込が認められる。}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|石棺俯瞰図 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|石棺展開図 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 北塚古墳 - 1988年（昭和63年）12月26日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会、2013年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:きたつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]
