{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[en:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[ja:北塚古墳]] [[sn:itazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitazuka Kofun}}

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:北塚古墳 up to rev 104518483
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 北塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
[[家形石棺]]開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 34|緯度秒 = 22.38 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 18|経度秒 = 55.30 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 組合式[[家形石棺]]（[[横口式石槨]]?） |出土品 = 鉄釘・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「北塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = |ラベル = 北塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''北塚古墳'''（きたづかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[福山市]][[駅家町]]服部永谷にある[[古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-C.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|家形石棺立断面図}}]] 広島県東部、[[神辺平野]]から北に入った、服部大池から北の谷に面する丘陵端に築造された古墳である。墳丘は削平で失われているほか、[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）に試掘調査が実施されている[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳]（福山市ホームページ）。。 元々の墳形は明らかでない。埋葬施設としては[[花崗岩]]製の組合式[[家形石棺]]（または[[横口式石槨]]の石槨部）が遺存する。6枚の切石を組み合わせた石棺で、長さ2.34メートル・幅1.41メートル。高さ0.56メートルを測る。蓋石は各辺が丸みを帯びた長方形で、縄掛突起が退化したような装飾を造り出す。出土品としては試掘の際の鉄釘・[[須恵器]]（坏）片がある。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半史跡説明板。（または7世紀前半{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}）頃と推定され、[[猪ノ子古墳]]（福山市加茂町下加茂）のような[[横口式石槨]]（石棺式石室）古墳の前段階に位置づけられる。 古墳域は[[1988年]]（昭和63年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|石棺横景 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|石棺蓋石
{{small|左側に縄掛突起状装飾を造り出す。}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|石棺内部
{{small|蓋石内面・側石小口側に彫込が認められる。}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|石棺俯瞰図 Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|石棺展開図 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 北塚古墳 - 1988年（昭和63年）12月26日指定[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（福山市教育委員会、2013年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] - 広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」 * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] - 福山市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:きたつかこふん}} [[category:広島県の古墳]] [[category:福山市の歴史]] [[category:広島県指定史跡]]

'''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町}}, {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Stone coffin, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of stone coffin
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[en:Kitazuka Kofun]] [[ja:北塚古墳]] [[sn:itazuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitazuka Kofun}} {{translated page|ja|北塚古墳|version=104518483|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩|12=draft}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama Plain|qq=}}. The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of [[:en:Granite|granite]], or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped stone coffin (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama Plain|qq=}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped stone coffin made of [[:en:Granite|granite]], or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). [[commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|location={{ill|Fukuyama|qid=Q661038}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.57288888888889|N|133.31536111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya, Fukuyama|qq=|1=Ekiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668203}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama Plain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of [[:en:Granite|granite]], or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). {{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of Sarcophagus
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{translated page|ja|北塚古墳|version=104518483|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:北塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kitazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q103982897}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|location={{ill|Fukuyama|WD=Q661038}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.57288888888889|N|133.31536111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya, Fukuyama|1=Ekiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668203}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of [[:en:Granite|granite]], or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of Sarcophagus
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{translated page|ja|北塚古墳|version=104518483|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:北塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kitazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q103982897}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|location={{ill|Fukuyama|WD=Q661038}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.57288888888889|N|133.31536111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya, Fukuyama|1=Ekiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668203}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of [[:en:Granite|granite]], or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of Sarcophagus

[[sn:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|location={{ill|Fukuyama|WD=Q661038}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.57288888888889|N|133.31536111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya, Fukuyama|1=Ekiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668203}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of [[:en:Granite|granite]], or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of Sarcophagus
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{translated page|ja|北塚古墳|version=104518483|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:北塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kitazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q103982897}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|location={{ill|Fukuyama|WD=Q661038}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.57288888888889|N|133.31536111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya, Fukuyama|1=Ekiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668203}}, [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], Japan. The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of [[:en:Granite|granite]], or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of [[:en:Granite|granite]], or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of Sarcophagus
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{translated page|ja|北塚古墳|version=104518483|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q103982897}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|location={{ill|Fukuyama|WD=Q661038}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.57288888888889|N|133.31536111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982897]] Wikidata name: Kitazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya, Fukuyama|1=Ekiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668203}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of Sarcophagus
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{translated page|ja|北塚古墳|version=104518483|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q103982897}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite|1=Granite}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, such as cups. {{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{translated page|ja|北塚古墳|version=104518483|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q103982897}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kitazuka Kofun]][[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Kitazuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|location={{ill|Fukuyama|qid=Q661038|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.57288888888889|N|133.31536111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Kitazuka Kofun''' (北塚古墳) is a kofun (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Ekiya, Fukuyama|ja|駅家町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekiya, Fukuyama|1=Ekiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11668203}}, {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|qid=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It is designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (top view)}}]] [[File:Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|House-shaped Sarcophagus (expanded view)}}]] The Kitazuka Kofun is situated in the eastern part of Hiroshima Prefecture, on the edge of a hill facing a valley north of the Hattori Reservoir in the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kambe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuyama Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}}. The mound itself has been leveled, and a trial excavation was conducted in 1982.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html 北塚古墳] (Fukuyama City official website). The original shape of the mound remains uncertain. The burial facility consists of a house-shaped Sarcophagus made of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=granite|1=Granite}}, or possibly the coffin section of a {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. The coffin is composed of six precisely cut stones, measuring 2.34 meters in length, 1.41 meters in width, and 0.56 meters in height. Its lid stone features a rounded rectangular shape, with decorations resembling degraded projections for securing ropes. Excavated items include iron nails and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, such as cups. The kofun is believed to have been built during the late 7th century,Historic Site Information Board. or possibly the early 7th century.{{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}} It represents an earlier phase of {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} development, exemplified by the {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} in Fukuyama. In 1988, the site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html 北塚古墳] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education). Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Sarcophagus, side view Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|Coffin lid stone
{{small|Left side shows rope-like projection decorations.}} Kitazuka Kofun, sekkan-5.jpg|Interior of Sarcophagus
{{small|Carved details visible on lid stone and inner surfaces.}} == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site: ''' Kitazuka Kofun - designated on December 26, 1988. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(References used for writing this article)}} * Historic Site Information Board (Fukuyama City Board of Education, 2013). * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=北塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=北塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|(Sources not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=北塚古墳試掘調査|title=福山市文化財年報19|publisher=福山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kitazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121090.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64159.html Kitazuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City website {{translated page|ja|北塚古墳|version=104518483|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:北塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q103982897}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
